AAPL.rationale: AAPL's +30% price gain over the past year was entirely driven by P/E expansion (27x to 35x) while TTM EPS declined from $6.13 to $6.08; even after a 14% pullback from the Dec peak, the stock remains historically expensive at 35x with flat earnings, declining iPhone revenue, and China weakness outweighing Services strength.
key_factors: P/E expanded from 27x (Apr 2024) to 35x (Mar 2025) while TTM EPS declined from $6.13 to $6.08 — entire price gain is multiple expansion, not earnings growth
Q1 FY2025 record revenue $124.3B (+4% YoY) but iPhone revenue declined 1% to $69.1B and China sales fell 11%
Services revenue grew 14% to $26.3B (21% of total), driving margin stability at 46.9% gross and 24.3% net
Stock 14% below Dec 2024 peak of $259 but still trades at historically elevated 35x P/E with 2.86% earnings yield

trace.final_explanation: openclaw_per_ticker: 30/30 tickers produced a view via openclaw.

AAPL.final_explanation: Apple remains a premium-valued stock where P/E expansion has run far ahead of earnings growth; Services strength is real but insufficient to justify 35x on flat TTM EPS with iPhone and China headwinds.

AMZN.final_explanation: AMZN's structural margin expansion has accelerated (OI from $37B to $69B, margin 6.4%→10.7%) while the stock has round-tripped to Apr 2024 levels, creating the cheapest P/E (34x) in over a year — tariff-driven selloff overdone relative to fundamental improvement.

CVX.final_explanation: CVX has rallied 15% since Jan 2025 to $167, pushing P/E to 17.2x — the top of its 1-year range — despite flat EPS ($9.72) and softening oil macro. The Hess arbitration (May ICC hearing) creates binary risk that is insufficiently discounted at this valuation.

IBM.final_explanation: IBM's valuation has become dangerously stretched after a year of multiple expansion on declining earnings — the AI narrative is fully priced at 39x P/E for a 1.4% revenue grower.

MCD.final_explanation: MCD trades at a rich 27.4x P/E on declining earnings with no near-term catalyst to justify the premium — the defensive bid has pushed valuation beyond fair value.

MMM.final_explanation: MMM's turnaround is real but the market has fully re-rated it — at 20.8x P/B and 19.5x P/E with flat revenue and 3.35x leverage, risk-reward is unfavorable for new money.

MRK.final_explanation: MRK remains a quality compounder at a discount valuation, but the Gardasil headwind and new tariff/litigation risks warrant a more cautious stance than the prior bullish call.

SHW.final_explanation: SHW: Quality compounder with expanding margins and reasonable valuation after pullback from highs; Suvinil acquisition adds emerging market growth vector.

TRV.final_explanation: TRV remains attractively valued at trough P/E despite record earnings — the structural underwriting improvement hasn't been fully priced in.

UNH.final_explanation: UNH offers moderate risk-adjusted reward at 18.4x forward P/E with 2025 guidance signaling earnings recovery, but DOJ investigation overhang and decelerating revenue growth justify a lower conviction than the prior Jan 1 view.

V.final_explanation: Visa has been a consistent underweight across prior decisions due to regulatory overhang, but the stock has rallied 26% anyway — the evidence now warrants upgrading to mildly positive as fundamentals (ROE 52%, accelerating revenue) have improved while the market correctly ignores unresolved litigation noise.
